elements.title: Amanda Coulson
elements.contents: ... that Amanda Coulson (pictured) worked full-time as an emergency dispatcher during her career as an amateur boxer?

elements.title: one of the first studies of Indigenous Australian literature
elements.contents: ... that the author of one of the first studies of Indigenous Australian literature only arrived in Australia as a doctoral student?

elements.title: a funerary inscription
elements.contents: ... that a funerary inscription from Campania commemorates a Jerusalem-born woman, likely enslaved during the First Jewish–Roman War, who died at the age of 25?

elements.title: Fidel Sánchez Hernández
elements.contents: ... that Fidel Sánchez Hernández, the president of El Salvador, personally commanded troops during the 1969 Football War?

elements.title: 1935 Free City of Danzig parliamentary election
elements.contents: ... that the 1935 Free City of Danzig parliamentary election was seen as a setback for the Nazi Party, even though it won an absolute majority of seats?

elements.title: Yasuko Agawa
elements.contents: ... that Yasuko Agawa was so sought after that she would sing at "five different jazz clubs in a single night"?

elements.title: 2024 League Two play-off final
elements.contents: ... that Crawley Town F.C. reached the 2024 League Two play-off final by winning their semi-final with an aggregate score of 8–1, the largest margin in EFL play-off history?

elements.title: deliberately avoided requesting delivery of the goods
elements.contents: ... that a Mexican government agency paid suppliers in full upfront, then deliberately avoided requesting delivery of the goods?

elements.title: Ig Nobel Prize
elements.contents: ... that winners of the Ig Nobel Prize are silenced by an eight-year-old girl repeating "Please stop. I'm bored" if their acceptance speeches go on for too long?
